paper.title: The origin of B-type runaway stars based on kinematics

extraction.summary: The paper investigates 39 B-type runaway stars via orbital traceback. All 39 stars are found to be bound to the Galaxy: 29 have trajectories entirely within the Galactic disk, and 10 have disk-passing orbits yet still trace back to the disk. The paper does not present any object as possibly unbound from the Milky Way.

method_chain.summary: LAMOST DR8 radial velocities combined with Gaia DR3 astrometry from the catalog of 547 early-type runaway stars by Guo et al. (2024).
Selection of 46 B-type runaway stars from the catalog, followed by removal of one BHB contaminant and six stars outside the PARSEC training grid, yielding a final sample of 39 stars.
Visual spectral subclassification of LAMOST LRS spectra using MKCLASS-based criteria.
Atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, [M/H], v sin i) derived with the Stellar Label Machine (SLAM) for Teff >= 15000 K, or adopted from Xiang et al. (2022) for Teff < 15000 K.
Stellar masses and ages estimated with a random-forest regression model trained on PARSEC 1.2S evolutionary tracks.
Distances obtained from the posterior distribution of geometric distances by Bailer-Jones et al. (2021).
Galactocentric positions and velocities computed adopting solar motion, R0 = 8.5 kpc, and Vc = 220 km/s.
Orbital traceback performed with GALPY using Monte Carlo simulations to propagate orbits backward in time and infer birthplaces.
Origin assessment via orbital morphology and cluster cross-matching to distinguish binary supernova scenario (BSS) and dynamical ejection scenario (DES).

candidate_groups_considered.description: The 39 B-type runaway stars listed in Table 1, selected from the LAMOST runaway catalog and analyzed with orbital traceback.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper does not present these objects as possibly unbound from the Galaxy. All 39 stars are concluded to be Galactic-disk-bound: 29 have trajectories entirely within the disk, and 10 are disk-passing yet still trace back to the disk. They are ordinary runaway stars, not HVS or unbound candidates.

source_refs.context: Abstract states all 39 stars trace back to the Galactic disk, with 29 confined and 10 disk-passing.
Results section explicitly states all 39 stars originate from within the Galactic disk.
Summary reiterates that all 39 stars are disk-bound, with no mention of unbound or escaping candidates.
